title: rosa viscaria var. dolorosa
intro: Rosa viscaria var. dolorosa, also known as the sorrel rose, is a type of rose plant that belongs to the genus Rosa, and is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rosa viscaria var. dolorosa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as a natural antiseptic. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems and respiratory issues.
